topic: carbonic maceration, fresh fruit, and low tannin
trigger_keywords: maceración carbónica en vinos tintos jóvenes
aromas de frutas frescas y poco tanino
carbonic maceration fresh fruit low tannin

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Whole intact berries are held in a carbon-dioxide-rich vessel before conventional fermentation is complete.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Intracellular fermentation develops distinctive fruity esters while limited crushing and skin extraction restrict tannin pickup.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The young red wine shows vivid fresh-fruit aromas, low tannin, and an approachable texture.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: The young red wine shows vivid fresh-fruit aromas, low tannin, and an approachable texture.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
